observations.recommendation:
1.7. We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate the outstanding checks
identified in bank reconciliation to the Municipal Treasurer for her
to send a written notice to the payees one month before the check
becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS
for LGUs; and
b. Municipal Accountant properly record/adjusts the reconciling items
on stale checks pursuant to Section 59 of NGAS Manual. A Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) be prepared for the stale check to revert the
cash in bank account.

2.15. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Designate a team or employees within the Municipal Treasury
Office through a memorandum. This team will be responsible
for preparing, maintaining, and updating the certified list of
taxpayers, which should include their names, property
descriptions, periods of arrears, and the amounts due based on
the Real Property Tax Account Register and the taxpayer index
card.
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal
Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers. This will ensure
an accurate and reliable basis for establishing receivables for
Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) at
the beginning of the year, thus guaranteeing the fair
presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
c. The Treasury Office to:
a) Continue conducting an information drive and tax
campaign to raise the awareness of its constituents
regarding the advance payment of the real property
taxes and to avail the discounts, encouraging taxpayers
to pay promptly and improving collection efficiency of
the Municipality on RPT/SET; and
b) Continue sending letters to delinquent taxpayers and
post notice of delinquency at the main entrance of the
municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and
conspicuous place in each Barangay of the local
government unit concerned. Also, the Municipality may
apply the available legal remedies to generate more
revenue that may be appropriated for the
implementation of additional programs and projects for
the benefit of its constituents.

3.17. To ensure that inventories are protected from misapplication or misuse,
we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Supply Officer the following:
a. To record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of
accounting and strictly adhere to the pro-forma accounting
entries on all purchased supplies and materials;
b. To maintain perpetual Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards
for each type of supplies and materials;
c. To conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock
cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly
accounted for;
d. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive
physical count of all types of inventory items semi-annually,
prepare the RPCI and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
for the first and second semesters, respectively. Also, invite a
representative from the COA to act as a witness in the physical
count; and
e. To ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented
in the financial statements.

4.15. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Re-organize the Inventory Committee in a memorandum or
Executive Order responsible for the conduct of a thorough and
complete physical count of all properties at least once a year and
subsequently prepare the RPCPPE using the proper format and
to submit a copy of the said reports to the Audit Team not later
than January 31 of every year;
b. Designate a Property Officer to maintain Property Cards for all
the PPEs of the LGU;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Ledger Cards and
be reconciled with the Property Cards of the Property Officer;
d. Direct the Property Officer, the Inventory Committee and the
Municipal Accountant to adhere with the guidelines and
procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to reflect reliable balances of PPE accounts.

4.16 Furthermore, we recommended that all issued property and equipment
with a unit cost below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00, that
are still included in the PPE accounts, be appropriately derecognized
from the PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated
May 31, 2022, and COA Circular No. 2024-006, dated March 14, 2024.

5.14 We have recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on
the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 be complied with.

5.15 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network; and
b. The Designated Property Officer, in coordination with the
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development
Officer, maintains a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and
keeps a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and their components.

6.14 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to trace the details of the differences
arising from the reconciliation of SCBAA and SFPer under
MOOE to provide the necessary disclosures and comply with
the disclosure requirements of IPSAS and the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs by adequately including a concise narrative on the
reconciling items to ensure that relevant information is available
to the intended users of the Financial Statements.
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to
maintain RAAOs in support of the actual amounts in the
SCBAA and the reconciliation with the amounts presented in
the SFPer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with
the required maintenance of complete and updated forms, registries and
reports pertaining to inventories.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require and instruct the:
(a) Inventory Committee/ Municipal Treasurer to complete the
conduct of the annual physical count of PPE; and
(b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) trace and
reconcile the variances noted between the accounting records and
the RPCPPE; and (ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing
of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information; and
(c) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining
unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines
and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
(a) cause the immediate remittance of the amount due to the various
Barangays and the PGNE to comply with the provisions of R.A. No.
7160; and
(b) exert best efforts in locating the documents pertaining to the
abnormal, dormant and undocumented balances and make the
necessary adjustments (if warranted) to reflect the accurate balance
of the Due to LGUs account in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:

a) MHRMO to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the computed total
money value of leave credits of all elected and regular employees that
will be the basis of the latter in recording in the books of accounts; and
b) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the total money value
of leave credits computed by the MHRMO by debiting Prior Period
Adjustment/ Terminal Leave Benefits and crediting Leave Benefits
Payable, in accordance with IPSAS 39 and COA Circular No. 2024-009.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE:
(a) direct the Licensing Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to
coordinate with their Information Technology personnel to enhance the
BPLS reports module by incorporating the taxpayer’s subsidiary ledgers
for Business Tax Accounts, thus ensuring accurate and efficient
business tax computation, collection processes, and monitoring; and
(b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct examination of
the books of accounts of business establishments by assigning
staff/personnel who are equipped with the necessary knowledge and
training in the examination of books of accounts to determine the
truthfulness of the declared gross sales/receipts used in the computation
of business taxes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue enhancing collection efforts
through a more aggressive taxpayer engagement and expanding
digital payment options; and (ii) enforce legal remedies in
accordance with Section 258 of R.A. No. 7160, particularly the
issuance of warrants of levy against delinquent properties; and
(b) Municipal Assessor to (i) request from the DAR a list of tenant
farmers who were awarded TCTs; and (ii) direct the tenant
farmers to declare their properties at the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the
Municipal Engineer and the Local Development Council to:

(a) properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to
development projects;
(b) regularly monitor the status and time frame of project
implementation and completion, respectively; and
(c) ensure that the use of the 20% DF is maximized and conformed with
the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and its implementing rules
and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) MHRMO to (i) consider prioritizing the filling-up of 39 open regular
vacant positions; (ii) indicate in the succeeding contracts of the JO
workers their job description and ensure that contracts are duly signed by
the JO workers; and
(b) MHRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the tasks
performed by the JO workers as indicated in the ARs are quantified and
with sufficient particularity.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit the lacking documentary
requirements on the three procurements of goods totaling ₱5,195,640.00; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) strengthen her review function on the
requisite documentation of all transactions; and (ii) maintain separate
RAAO for LDDRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the GFPS to further
improve its compliance on GAD regulations by:
(a) vigilantly overseeing the execution of GAD PPAs to ensure their efficient
and effective completion within the approved budget and specified
timeframe; and
(b) conforming to the guidelines for reporting changes in the GPB to DILG.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to continue their practice of proper withholding and
timely remittance of the full amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth,
and Pag-IBIG.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the MLSB to:
(a) use the prescribed SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 in
compliance with Item 5.2.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017; and
(b) submit the quarterly and annual SEF Utilization Reports to the DepEd
CO, and furnish copies to the SB concerned and Regional Offices of the
DBM and DILG to fully comply with the requirements of Item 6.1 of the
DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We commended the LCE and the MLSB for properly monitoring the
implementation of all PPAs and ensuring that the total budgeted amount for
SEF projects was implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery
of targeted services and benefits to the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) concerned offices to exert best efforts to locate the missing DV
amounting to ₱1,041,478.39, and if the efforts failed, reconstruct
immediately the DV and supporting documents to support the
payment made; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence in releasing checks with
a view of securing the safety and custody of financial transaction
documents subject of payment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct:
(a) every end-user units to be more prudent in conducting reasonable
price canvass of the items to be procured; and
(b) the Chairman and Members of the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to diligently perform their respective duties and functions,
particularly in conducting evaluation of the price canvass submitted
by the end-users and ensuring that the prices stated therein are
favorable to the government, with a view of preventing the
recurrence of overpricing to avoid wastage of government resources.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Vice
Mayor and SB Officials:
(a) ensure that DBM NBC No. 563 and DBM BC No. 2007-1 be strictly
complied with;
(b) explain/justify the excessive expenditures and the hiring of
unqualified RP; and
(c) submit the Schedule of Topics discussed in the three-day TBA.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strategize effective
measures on how to minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays in
the submission of accounts;
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) require other officials concerned in the
processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect prompt
submission thereof to her office; and (ii) ensure the submission to the
Audit Team of the paid DVs and supporting documents, Trial Balances,
and BRS within the prescribed period; and
(c) Municipal Budget Officer to promptly submit the monthly SAOBs to the
Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile RAAOs and SAAOB regularly, to warrant the accuracy of the amounts presented in the SCBAA; and

Ensure that LBE Form No. 2 is duly accomplished before making any adjustment/s in the appropriated amounts in the approved annual budget.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to complete the review of the identified reconciling items and effect the necessary journal entries in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account and its corresponding income and expense account to be presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipal Government.

Instruct the BPLO and the Municipal Treasury to have a repository in its BPLS, the complete record of each person/ entity paying revenue per Section 7A.08. of the Revenue Code which includes among others the Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for the preceding year and the Certified Income Tax Return Copy.  

Require the BPLO and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as to the amount of sales/ receipts derived by the business entities from the Municipality and the barangays to have a reference of the actual sales/ receipts of the entities from the LGUs within Natividad alone. Remit all taxes withheld, premium contributions, and loan repayment intact on or before the deadline as provided in the rules and regulations set by the BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid penalties, surcharges, and interests from delayed remittances; and
Reconcile the Due to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts with the actual amounts withheld based on the filed tax returns, statement of account, and loan amortization schedule from respective agencies, to identify the under and over-remittances incurred from prior years until the current year.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that would require accountable officers to settle or liquidate immediately their past due accounts; and (b) if conditions and circumstances on writing-off the accounts are met, initiate the procedures for filing a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Moreover, the existing regulations on CAs should be strictly observed at all times to avoid the state of dormancy.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
observe the provision under Section 20 of the NGAS Manual by using the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables as at year-end to support the integrity of the accounts.
Municipal Treasurer to:
timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, particulars of the property, location, amount due and collectible for the applicable year and other pertinent information; and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET Receivable in the books of accounts of the Municipality at the beginning of the year; and
introduce collection strategies that would significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the Municipality; and institute remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the (a) Inventory Committee to exert best effort to complete the physical count of all inventory items of the Municipal Government, prepare the RPCSP and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated forms, registries and reports pertaining to inventories.

For the deficiencies discussed above, we recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Inventory Committee and Municipal Treasurer to (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records;
Municipal Accountant and  Municipal Treasurer  to (i) complete the necessary procedures enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the missing/unlocated PPE items and unidentifiable balances; and (ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information; and
Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to:
make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and
submit the Notice of Suspension and Resumption of Work for the project Construction of Health Station in Barangay Piñahan to determine its justifiability.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) cause the immediate return of the unutilized and/ or non- moving funds to the respective Source Agencies (NGAs) or the Bureau of Treasury in observance of Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and (b) consider the adoption of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on requesting for authority to write-off of dormant accounts to clear the books of any unaccounted balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) cause the immediate remittance of the RPT and SET shares of the barangays and the Provincial Government, as well as the excess funds in the 2007 Barangay Election to the COMELEC in the total amount of ₱1,589,152.54; and (b) consider the adoption of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on requesting for authority to Write-Off of dormant accounts amounting to ₱638,657.43, to clear the books of any unaccounted balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of proper withholding and timely remittance of the full amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the (a) MDRRMO to (i) be more cautious in the preparation of LDRRMFIP to avoid realignment of funds during the execution phase of the formulated PPAs and to maximize its implementation and fund utilization; (ii) identify and incorporate under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the specific PPAs to be implemented and charged against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF; and (iii) submit the lacking documentary requirements on the two procurements of goods totaling ₱5,198,373.00; and (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of all transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Local Development Council (a) to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects; (b) regularly monitor the time frame and status of project implementation; and (c) ensure that the use of the 20% DF is maximized and conforms with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and its IRR.

We recommended the LCE to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to be prudent in exercising their functions and ensure compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in all procurement activities, particularly on (a) provision of safety and health program for project workers and (b) the submission of Contractor’s Affidavit on the payment of laborers and materials.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit immediately the two DVs and supporting documents totaling ₱461,733.25 and to (b) strictly observe the timely submission of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) require other officials concerned in the processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect prompt submission thereof to her office; and (b) ensure the submission to the Audit Team of the paid DVs, Trial Balances, Financial Statements with supporting documents, and Bank Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the members of the Municipal LSB to properly monitor the implementation of all PPAs and ensure that the total budgeted amount for SEF projects is implemented/ utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MSWMB to (a) continue to implement and enforce meaningful and relevant PPAs that will help promote the proper waste management to protect the environment and the health and safety of the population; and (b) strictly enforce the at-source segregation of wastes thru the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”

In view of the above, we recommended and Management agreed to:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct examination on the books of accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel who are equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination of books of accounts to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid and to collect the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of the RA 7160; and
instruct the Licensing Officer to fully enforce the submission of Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax Return from taxpayers to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as provided by Section 2F.04 of the Revenue Code.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Person to:
(a) 	create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GAD Plas and Budget by using the templates provided in PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September 19, 2018; and
(b) 	timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We have recommended that the Management:

Instruct the Accountant to issued Letter of Demand to Accountable Officers and Employees whose cash advances is not liquidated within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and enforce immediate liquidation of cash advances.

And instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the salary of personnel with unliquidated cash advances for failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period as provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2022 –p. 30 | The Municipal Accountant is sending Demand Letter to overdue Cash Advances, prompting active personnel to regularly liquidating cash advances.



Prompt liquidation of active permanent LGU personnel, doesn’t require withholding of salary.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to (1) prepare the comparative consolidated financial statements which includes the effect of the changes in accounting policies, if any, and corrections of prior period errors; and (2) disclose in the Notes the effects of the restatement to be made on the accounts presented in the financial statements, pursuant to IPSAS 1 and 3 to achieve comparability of the financial reports of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 43 | The Municipal Accountant submitted comparative financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management thru the BAC to require the end-user units to prepare their respective PPMPs for their different PAPs and submit to the Budget Office for evaluation in order to ensure consistency with the Procuring Entity’s ensuing year budget proposal.  The PPMPs shall be forwarded to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into an indicative APP and to the BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement modality. These APP and PPMP as part of its Procurement Planning and Budgeting Linkage shall be in compliance to Sections 7.3.2 and 7.3.4 of Rule II of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  The APP shall then be submitted to the GPPB as provided in Section 7.3.5 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 51 | The Annual Procurement Plan for 2023 was emailed copy furnish the Government Procurement Policy Board or GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management thru the BAC and the Secretariat to post Notices of Award, Contracts and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS of all procurements thru Competitive Bidding and Alternative Methods of procurment to ensure transparency principle of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 55 | The BAC Secretariat is already posting the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS for transparency.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the MPDC, to (a) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to secure MOA with private lot owners; (b) ensure that any agreed conditions for the use of the private lot are beneficial to the Municipality; (c) provide a copy of the secured MOA to the Audit Team; and (d) refrain from implementing projects on private lots without a MOA or a Deed of Donation to avoid any future legal implications which may expose the government projects to risk of loss.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 66 | The Municipal Government secured Deed of Donation from the respective lot owners.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management thru SB prioritize to revisit and review the taxes, fees and charges provided in 2007 Municipal Revenue Code and legislate an ordinance updating such taxes, fees and charges.  Once updated, the Municipal Treasurer Office will be able to collect higher and appropriate amount which may eventually use to finance priority projects and programs of the Municipality.  The increase shall at least equal to the adjusted current value of the taxes provided sixteen years ago, considering inflation and decrease in purchasing power of the peso.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 72 | The Sanggunian Bayan enacted Municipal Ordinance No. 88-2023-An Ordinance Updating the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality of Natividad, Province of Pangasinan dated June 26, 2023 and approved by the Local Chief Executive on July 6, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that Management submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents for review within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to have a timely review and evaluation process on the technical aspects, and its compliance to documentary requirements, procurement and other applicable governing laws, rules and regulations.  Moreover, having a timely review prevents entering into transaction as among those irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant, and unconscionable expenditures subject to disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 75 | The BAC is submitting contracts, purchase orders and its supporting documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Engineer to (a) prepare project signboard/billboard within the construction site visible to the public; and (b) ensure the implementation of the same by placing the project signboards/billboards within the project location before the start of any projects in compliance with the specifications provided under Section 2 of the COA Circular No. 2013-004; and (c) inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award or before the start of the PPA that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted pursuant to Section 3.1 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 –p. 86 | The Municipal Engineer see to it that signboard was posted in every infrastructure project of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our previous years’ audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) 

(a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of long outstanding cash advances granted to officials and employees; and 

(b)  continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to:

introduce collection strategies that would significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the Municipality; and institute remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, particulars of the property, location, amount due and collectible for the applicable year and other pertinent information; and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivable in the books of accounts of the Municipality at the beginning of the year; 


The Municipal Accountant to: 

observe the provision under Section 20 of the NGAS Manual by using the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF receivables at the beginning of the year; and


reconcile the balances RPT and SET Receivables as at year-end to support the integrity of the accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

a.	enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and updated property record, reconcile them periodically and settle the established variance in the property records;


b.	instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) reconstruct the records of Land account to prove the validity of recognizing the true untitled lots in the books; and 

(ii) comply with instructions of COA Circular 2015-008 respecting the preparation and maintenance of records for infrastructure projects; and

c.	instruct the Municipal Treasurer (i) to undertake disposal of unserviceable fixed assets to prevent further deterioration and decline in their value and to generate additional income from their sale and 

(ii) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in registering the two lots in favor of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:

a. MHRO to

i. assess prudently the need to engage the services of consultants and in case there is really a need, engage only the experts in their respective fields and submit the Curriculum vitae, contracts and accomplishment reports of the hired consultants for reference in audit;

ii. justify the contracts of the consultants whose services were only duplication of the functions of regular employees or whose works could be performed by existing plantilla personnel; and

iii. observe rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants; and

b. Municipal Accountant ensure that payments for consultancy services are supported with adequate documentation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to 

(a) continue the effective and efficient implementation of the projects; and 

(b) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:

a.	the MDRRMC to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the specific PPAs to be charged against the unexpended previous year’s LDRRMF; and

b.	all concerned officials to ensure that all projects and programs in the LDRMMIP are properly monitored and implemented.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the:

MHO staff to

exert best efforts in implementing its COVID-19 Vaccination Program and intensify the information campaign to the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine benefits to achieve the Herd Immunity as a way to fight the said virus; and 

render an Inventory Report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources for recording and disclosure purposes; and henceforth,

Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the donated vaccines to the Municipality based on the Inventory Report submitted by the MHO staff.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

a.	direct the MPDO, MSWDO, MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer to continue to observe the time frame of scheduled implementation/completion of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries; and 

b.	on dormant accounts, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) review all dormant accounts under the trust funds; 

(ii) retrieve their agreements and other documents; and 

(iii) determine whether the unutilized fund balance could be legitimately used by the Municipality or seek the authority from the donor on the possible utilization of the unexpended fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the LSB conduct the proper monitoring with respect to efficient execution and maximum utilization of the annual SEF budget with the view of accomplishing fully the predetermined programs and projects in furtherance and in harmony with the national government’s goals relevant to basic education.

We recommended that LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher for the cancellation of the checks and accordingly, for the restoration of cash equivalent of the stale checks and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts.

            We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer on the notification of payees for replacement and immediate negotiation of stale checks.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert extra efforts and improve their compliance on reports submission to curtail the incurrence of delays.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the prompt preparation and submission of the BRS to the Office of the Auditor within the required period for timely review and verification of the monthly reported cash in bank balances.

We recommended and both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of proper withholding and timely remittance of amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes on compensation and value added taxes, percentage taxes & expanded taxes from government money payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes that can be used in the implementation of various programs and projects.

We recommended that the LCE direct the 

(a) accountable officials and employees of the Municipality to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant, to submit the necessary supporting documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the above-mentioned reciprocal accounts which must be equal at all times and record reciprocal accounts transaction in a simultaneous manner to assure that asset and liability are recorded in books to comply with IN9 of IPSAS 1 requiring faithful representation.
observations.managementAction: Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds are not yet reconciled

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management thru the Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and its subsequent issuances/ usage. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer/ General Service Officer or designated personnel as the case maybe, is recommended to use property tags or stock cards for monitoring of inflow and outflow inventory that can be used in the reconciliation with the ledger.  With the above recommendation takes place, the municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: There some transactions where procurement of Materials and Supplies Inventories are recorded expensed outright.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended Management the following:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records particularly on all on-going projects that will be classified as part of Construction in Progress to have an accurate and reliable presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.

The Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the procedure provided in Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS by reclassifying the completed projects from CIP account to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that management of LGU Natividad enforce regularly that all procurement of goods and services be paid by checks directly to suppliers/ contractors/ service providers except for petty disbursements out of petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 and to comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23 – 2014 to avoid liabilities and penalties arising from non-compliance as clearly stipulated in the same BIR Revenue Memorandum Order.
observations.managementAction: There are some but minimal reimbursement that came to the auditor’s attention during the audit of the CY 2021 financial transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following to Management:

1.	Require the concerned Municipal Office to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor; and

2.	Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended Management to stop issuance of multiple PPEs to officials and employees pursuant to the provisions of Section 4 of the Republic Act No. 6713, Section 2 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive and unconscionable expenditures which may be disallowed in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We also recommend Management to immediately require the officials and employees concerned to return to the Property Officer issued mobile phones, laptops and other PPE issued to each aforementioned officials in excess of one unit as the provision of these PPE of the same kind/ similar nature in excess of one unit is considered excessive pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003. If the official/ employee could not present and return the excessive issued PPE because it has been lost, the official/ employee shall pay the equivalent amount of the PPE.

The Municipal Treasurer require his collecting agents to prepare and submit daily the Report of Collections and Deposits which will be the basis for recording in the Cashbook and General Ledger.
The Cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer and General Ledger of the Municipal Accountant to be updated on a daily basis and reconcile at least at month-end or as the need arises to ensure accuracy and reliability of its presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended Management to have a detailed disclosure of its Due from Other Funds, Due from GOCCs, Other Receivables, Accounts Payable, Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs, Due to Other Funds and Other Payables accounts as required by IPSAS 1 and 3 to achieve the objectives of general purpose financial reporting in the public sector which is to provide information useful for decision making, and to demonstrate the accountability of the entity for the resources entrusted to it by: (a) providing information about the sources, allocation and uses of financial resources; (b) providing information about how the entity financed its activities and met its cash requirements; and (c) providing information that is useful in evaluating the entity’s ability to finance its activities and to meet its liabilities and commitments.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the above-mentioned reciprocal accounts which must be equal at all times and record reciprocal accounts transaction in a simultaneous manner to assure that asset and liability are recorded in books to comply with IN9 of IPSAS 1 requiring faithful representation.

We recommended that the Management:
Create Inventory Committee and immediately conduct and complete the physical inventory and warrant full compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the existence, condition and accountability assertion of the Municipality on Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) in the Financial Statements.
Renew the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of PPEs found in the custody of officials/ employees to re-establish accountability.
Be guided by Section 374 to 377 of Republic Act 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code on the accountability provisions of government properties.

the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
the General Services Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

To record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and materials and use inventory tags or stock cards for monitoring.
To conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for and
To ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We recommended Management to submit monthly financial reports on time, particularly the Financial Statements, Disbursements Vouchers, Cash and Check Disbursement Journals, Report of Collections and Deposits, Cash Receipts Journals and supporting documents in compliance to the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 100 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents for review within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to have a timely review and evaluation process on the technical aspects, and its compliance to documentary requirements, procurement and other applicable governing laws, rules and regulations.
Moreover, having a timely review prevents entering into transaction as among those irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant, and unconscionable expenditures subject to disallowance.

We recommended Management thru the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to reconcile their financial records and reports on the DRRM funds. They shall maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for transfers of the LGU’s unutilized DRRM to the special trust fund to lessen incurrence of errors and always disclose to the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance using the required format to establish faithful representation of accounts as mandatory requirement by IPSAS 1.
Moreover, we recommended Management to transfer the unobligated/ unspent appropriation balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management:
Include the updating of the Municipal Revenue Code in the Executive-Legislative Agenda in order to have prioritization of the next term of Administration.
Sangguniang Bayan prioritize to revisit and review the taxes, fees and charges provided in 2007 Municipal Revenue Code and legislate an ordinance updating such taxes, fees and charges.  Once updated, the Municipal Treasurer Office will be able to collect higher and appropriate amount which may eventually use to finance priority projects and programs of the Municipality.  The increase shall at least to equal to the adjusted current value of the taxes provided fourteen years ago, considering inflation and decrease in purchasing power of the peso.
Management to provide budget for its enactment and publication in the 2023 Municipal Budget.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the Executive and Legislative Departments of the Local Government of Natividad shall coordinate and cooperate with each other and see to it that the Annual Budget of the municipality could be enacted through an ordinance within the prescribed period set in Section 319 of the Local Government Code of 1991 so that it will not fall into a re-enacted budget thus hampering the full implementation  of the much needed development programs/ projects/ activities of the Municipality.  In this time of pandemic every elected official and employee of government shall work together helping the constituents affected to pass through in these trying times.  Serving our people should be prioritized beyond politics and personal preference.

We recommended to the Management the following:
stop the practice of charging procured PPE items to MOOE contrary to of Section 4 of PD No. 1445, Section 336 of the Local Government Code and Budget Operations Manual for LGUs.  The needed PPE items of all Offices/ Departments/ Divisions as well as projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund and 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund which are development projects with more than one-year life span shall be properly incorporated as CO in the Municipal Annual Budget and the procurement for the said projects shall be charged against the appropriation for CO instead of MOOE pursuant to the aforementioned guidelines.
thru the Local Finance Committee that in their preparation of the Annual Budget and Annual Investment Plan, CO and MOOE are clearly defined and identified in order that juggling in the budget will be prevented.
stop procurement of PPE or Capital Outlay, in disguise of MOOE in case that the LGU is operating on a reenacted budget pursuant to Section 323 of the Local Government Code.
properly recognized in the books of accounts the PPE items listed in the table.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the aforementioned supporting documents to establish regularity and propriety of the claims.  For succeeding transactions of the same nature, he shall see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment. Strict compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation be always adhered for all claims against government funds.

We recommended that Management:
The SB member/ Head of Department who is in-charge of distributing purchased goods or property donated by the Municipality for the barangay shall ensure that an Acknowledgement Receipt for Donated Supplies and Materials (ARDPESM) was duly accomplished by the barangay to establish their accountability.  This shall be coordinated with the PB and the Barangay Treasurer concerned and a copy for the Barangay, the Municipal Accountant and the COA Audit Team shall be prepared and duly submitted.
The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that materials/ property purchased by the Municipality for the barangay shall be also recorded in the barangay books of accounts.
The SB member/ Head of Department in-charge of distributing purchased goods or property donated by the Municipality for the barangay/ an entity shall ensure that the goods/ property do not contain the name of a Sangguniang Bayan member or any public official concerned pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the (a) accountable
officers to improve further their compliance on the reporting/liquidation of cash
advances; and (b) Municipal Accountant, to intensify collection measures or
institute appropriate remedy that would require the accountable officers to settle or
liquidate their past due accounts; and (c) if conditions and circumstances on writing
off the accounts would be present, direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the
procedures for the application of the request for write-off under COA Circular
2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) create an
Inventory Committee and require it to conclude the inventory count for CY 2021;
(b) submit the related reports to the Office of the Auditor and reconcile the
resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; and (c) direct the
Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete PPE records such as Property Cards for
the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to (a) direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (i) review all dormant accounts under the
trust funds, (ii) retrieve their agreements and other documents, and (iii) determine
whether the unutilized fund balance could be legitimately used by the Municipality
or seek the authority from the donor on the possible utilization of the unexpended
fund balance; and (b) instruct the Heads of the Offices concerned to ensure the
timely implementation of the projects and conduct the appropriate reporting that
would guide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer on the eventual
closure or liquidation of the accounts in the sponsoring agency’s books.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) continue to
comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121; (b) require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents for the
financial assistance to COVID 19 infected individuals; and (c) require the MDRRM
Officer to regularly submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to continue to
observe the guidelines on the 20% development fund and institute the Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to strictly monitor the
status of implementation of all projects and ensure that their completion are within
the contract period for the immediate utilization of the intended beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations that:

Sanggunian Bayan prioritize to revisit and review the taxes, fees and charges provided in 2007 Municipal Revenue Code and legislate an ordinance updating such taxes, fees and charges.  Once updated, the Municipal Treasurer Office will be able to collect the right and appropriate amount which may eventually use to finance priority projects and programs of the municipality.  The increase shall at least to equal to the adjusted current value of the taxes provided fourteen years ago, considering inflation and decrease in purchasing power of the peso.

Management to provide budget for its enactment and publication in the 2022 Municipal Budget.

We recommended Management to adhere to the provisions of Section 61 of PD No. 1445 on authorization of government expenditures particularly on gasoline expenses and to the internal controls on fuel consumption of government motor vehicles to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant and unconscionable gasoline expenditures which may be disallowed in audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We also recommended Management the following:

Assign a personnel in-charge of issuing approved gas slip apart from an approved driver’s trip ticket which should be serially numbered to be presented to the gasoline station when procuring gasoline.  The personnel shall also have a complete list of all municipal motor vehicles and with the list of corresponding maximum fuel tank capacity of each vehicle as a guide/reference in approving the number of liters in the gas slip/Driver’s Trip Ticket before an authorized driver can procure gasoline.  The approved Driver’s Trip Tickets, Gas Issue Slip/RIV, Charge Invoice and its summary together with the supplier’s billing statement will serve as supporting documents for the disbursement/ payments for the fuel expenses.  

Instruct the Accountant that the Municipality shall only pay the number of liters approved by the Municipality per Gas Issue Slip in case there is an excess in the actual number of liters issued by the supplier per Billing Statement.

Adequate monitoring and control shall be exercised by the Municipal officials concerned ensuring that the Municipality shall only pay expenses that are not irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant and unconscionable COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended Management thru the Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year pursuant to item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five (5) years.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the rendition of accounts and financial reports and submit the accounts and accounting reports within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the requirement of Section 286 of Republic Act No. 7160 and cause the automatic release/remittance of RPT and SET shares to the respective local government units.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with various laws and regulations such as DSWD Memorandum No. 04 Series of 2020 and RA 11469 in the disbursements of government funds and in the implementation of government projects, programs and activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/BAC Chairman/Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with COA  Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006 dated February 12, 2009 and May 18, 1995, respectively on the furnishing of the COA Audit Team with the required copies of contract and purchase orders with their supporting documents, as well as notices of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection on a sampling basis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) through the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Office (i) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and

 (ii) optimally utilize budget for Development Fund through realignment of unutilized balances to other eligible projects; and 

(b) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the implementation of the unimplemented project for the benefit of its intended users.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure the implementation of GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved GAD plans and budget and 

(b) refrain from including programs which cannot be identified with GAD.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue the implementation of the program/projects/activities for the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management to strengthen the disaster and mitigation measures of the Municipality at times of calamities.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of proper withholding and timely remittance of amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We have reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. 

 Require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and

Compliance with Item IN9 of IPSAS No. 1 is strictly recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the rendition of accounts and financial reports and submit the accounts and accounting reports within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/BAC Chairman/Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with COA  Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006 dated February 12, 2009 and May 18, 1995, respectively on the furnishing of the COA Audit Team with the required copies of contract and purchase orders with their supporting documents, as well as notices of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection on a sampling basis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure the implementation of GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved GAD plans and budget and (b) refrain from including programs which cannot be identified with GAD.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of proper withholding and timely remittance of amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the above-mentioned reciprocal accounts which must be equal at all times and record reciprocal accounts transaction in a simultaneous manner to assure that asset and liability are recorded in books to comply with IN9 of IPSAS 1 requiring faithful representation.
In the written reply of the Management, the Office of the Municipal Accountant has already identified and adjusted the transactions affecting the reciprocal accounts.

We recommend Management thru the Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and its subsequent issuances/ usage. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer/ General Service Officer or designated personnel as the case maybe, is recommended to use property tags or stock cards for monitoring of inflow and outflow inventory that can be used in the reconciliation with the ledger.  With the above recommendation takes place, the municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.
In the written reply submitted by Management, the Accounting Office has justified that they are aware of the policy under Section 114 Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual that purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method.
The Accounting Office recorded the purchase as an outright expense to simplify the recording process, since the delivered inventory items (office supplies and medicines) are directly issued to offices concerned.  Moreover, corresponding requisition and issue slip is issued and attached to the disbursement voucher to support the issuance of inventory.  In addition, Management commented that the Municipality, does not have General Services Office to maintain stock cards or department to monitor issuance of inventory of supplies or medicines and the accounting office does not have any means in monitoring the issued inventory.
As for compliance with the existing policy/provision under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual, the Accounting Unit tries its best to properly adopt the existing policy.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team reiterates that the rules stated in Section 114, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS remains the same and that is to record the purchases under the Asset Method for a better control of inventories against misapplication or misuse.
We also remind Management that the same manual states that in the absence of a designated GSO, the Municipal Treasurer shall maintain the stock cards and property cards

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, and require them to reconcile these records regularly to have a verifiable, accurate and reliable PPE balances as presented in the Statement of Financial Position.
The Municipal Accountant had already started a system for the Property Ledger Cards since 2019. The Municipal Treasurer, also maintaining Property Cards, however, due to workloads, he cannot duly maintain all through out.  Nonetheless, Management agreed to comply.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The nature of the items purchased was in the nature of PPE and it is classified to belong to Solar Street Light System, although these function individually.   With regards to control, if it is already transferred to recipient barangays, deed of donations or acknowledgement receipts of barangays have to be accomplished and submitted as basis for recognizing  the cost of the Solar Street Light System in the Books of the recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that OIC-Municipal Accountant make necessary notices to concerned offices for the submission of SSMI together with the corresponding RIS which shall be the basis in preparing JEV in the dropping of the subject inventory items. 

Likewise, exert more effort to trace all the pertinent documents pertaining to prior year’s transactions and make necessary adjustment to present the accurate balances of the Inventory accounts.

We recommended that the management 
(a) exert effort in locating documents on the subject accounts for proper accounting and collection/liquidation thereof; and 
(b) file the request for authority to write off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 if all the conditions and requirements provided therein are complied.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that management furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract/purchase orders and their supporting documents, as well as notices of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the property officer to procure their common-use supplies from the Procurement Services of the Department of Budget and Management in compliance with the Republic Act No. 9184, the Government Procurement Reform Act and in pursuance with the DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) direct the Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund only to eligible expenditures; and 

(b) advise the Local Development Council to perform due diligence in the review of the budget to ensure the proper funding source of identified high-impact or capital expenditure projects for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management utilize Gender and Development Fund on programs/projects and activities in accordance with the mandates of Republic Act No. 7192 and PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-1 to all government agencies to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count and prepare the RPCPPE necessary for control and monitoring of PPEs.  In effect, the accuracy, validity, existence and actual condition could be ascertained and render the account not doubtful.
The Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of Supply Officer, and the Municipal Accountant start maintaining their respective property and ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment to record deliveries of properties in the ensuing year.
And lastly, the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the book records with the inventory report as internal control over the PPEs of the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the procedure provided in Section 50 of the NGAS Manual by re-classifying the completed projects from CIP account to the respective Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) account.

Further, compliance with IPSAS IN9 is strictly recommended.

We have recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Further, compliance with IPSAS IN9 is strictly recommended.

We have recommended that the management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of CAs particularly on the prohibition of granting  additional cash advance to officials/employees unless the previous cash advances was settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.  Moreover, the Municipality shall only grant cash advances to its employees either for Regular or Special Cash Advances following the provisions of Item 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We have recommended that the Municipality increase the Petty Cash Fund based on the approved estimates of petty expenses for one (1) month and the Petty Cash Fund Custodian shall strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 that prescribed the manner and timing of PCF replenishment.

We have recommended that the officials concerned shall strictly adhere to the prescribed period relative to the timely submission of monthly reports so that the Audit Team could also perform the timely review and evaluation of the agency’s financial transactions and communicate the audit results to the management for decision making.

We have recommended that Municipal Accountant see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds is strictly recommended.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 21 of RA No. 10121 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF.  Likewise, instruct the Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year.

We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan to revisit and review the tax, fees and charges provided in 2007 Municipal Revenue Code and legislate an ordinance updating such tax, fees and charges.  Once updated, the Municipal Treasurer Office will be able to collect the right and appropriate amount which may eventually use to finance priority projects and programs of the municipality.  The increase shall at least to equal to the adjusted current value of the taxes provided twelve years ago, considering inflation and decrease in purchasing power of the peso.
Moreover, provide budget for its enactment and publication in the 2020 Municipal Budget.

We have reiterated the previous year recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers in order for the Accounting Unit to have proper basis in setting up the amount of RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year to present fairly the balances of these accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to liquidate to respective National Government Agencies the fund transfer pertaining to implemented/ completed projects and programs.  Liquidation documents shall be in accordance with  the provision of COA Circular 2012-001 Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction with respect to the clarification provided in COA Corcular 2017-002.

Further, compliance with IPSAS IN9 is strictly recommended.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions in the books of accounts in accordance with accounting rules and regulations. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that transactions are charged to the proper accounts in order to show the correct account balances in the financial statements.

Info. & Comm. Tech. Equipment	P 126,500.00

Buildings					      99,697.05

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record inventory using the prescribed perpetual method of accounting on all purchased supplies and use inventory tags or stock cards for monitoring. Likewise, conduct regular reconciliation of the ledger and the stock cards to ensure that all purchases and issuances are properly accounted for.  With the above recommendation takes place, the municipality have the assurance that inventories are safeguarded against risk of misapplication or misuse and ensure the reliability and accuracy of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer, to thoroughly review payroll of employees availing loans before certifying payroll/salary document requirements for loan applications.  She has also be aware of the minimum net take home pay for the ensuing year which is integrated in the GAA and usually passed and approved at the end of the year.  Only employees whose salaries are P 5,000.00 and above after deducting the loan applying for shall be allowed to avail of loan.
