observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a copy of the report to the audit team in order to ascertain its accuracy, validity, existence and actual condition. To facilitate the completion of physical count of properties, the inventory committee may consider performing physical count of newly acquired properties before moving to those that were acquired in the prior years. Inventory tagging should also be made in order to prevent double counting.
We have further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of Supply Officer, and the Municipal Accountant start maintaining their respective property and ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment to record deliveries of properties in the ensuing year.

We have recommended to management that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers in order for the Accounting Unit to have proper basis in setting up the amount of RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year to present fairly the balances of these accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the municipality strictly comply with the provisions of  COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016  and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 on the required analysis of dormant accounts by  performing  the regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and determine the concerned debtors, accountable officers for strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts; and provide for allowance for impairment for dormant receivables and cash advance accounts in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under PPSAS 29. Compute/determine   impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards.

We recommended that the management strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances. It is further recommended that:
No additional cash advance be given to officials/employees unless the previous cash advances was settled and the liquidating papers were submitted to the auditor for post-audit;
Exert all possible remedies to collect and demand liquidation of cash advances;
Initiate the filing of appropriate Administrative Proceedings for Gross Neglect of Duty to those who failed to render account of their accountabilities after demand or Simple Neglect of Duty to those who failed to liquidate their cash advances in full within the prescribed periods pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12, s. 2012; and
Request for write-off on the following applicable situations:
Death of accountable officer/ employee/ debtor
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/ employee/ debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her.
Incapacity to pay/insolvency.
Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the management to collect receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers

We have reiterated that the accountable officers adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 73 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I and Section 101 of PD 1445 to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in their custody are kept diligently.
We have also recommended that the Accountable Officer exhaust all means to trace the identity and whereabouts of the person responsible for the missing accountable forms and finally settle the issue.

We have reiterated that the Municipal Treasurer to require all Collecting officers to remit their collections and accomplish their Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at close of each business day pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual of New Government Accounting System, Vol. 1 since cash collections are vulnerable to loss, misapplication, and/or unauthorized use.

We have recommended that the municipal treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Regular recording with due diligence of the financial transactions in the cashbook by the Municipal Treasurer is also recommended.
We have reiterated that the Petty Cash Fund Custodian shall strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 that prescribed the manner and timing of PCF replenishment.

We have recommended that the Accounting Unit should record the purchase of all supplies and materials as inventory following the perpetual method of accounting. Issuances thereof should be recorded as they take place using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) which shall be submitted by the Supply Officer.

We have recommended that the management ensures that all expenses incurred shall be duly authorized; adequately funded and documented; properly recorded; actually incurred; and appropriately and consistently classified.  Appropriating Capital Outlay Budget for each office is recommended.
We have recommended that the management refund the training expenses of the consultant charged to government funds. Also, refrain from charging irregular expenditures to government resources.

We have reiterated that management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the procurement of goods and services such that competitive public bidding be adopted in all its procurement activities The Bids and Awards Committee, who has the responsibility for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR, should strictly observe the provisions on the use of alternative mode of procurement before resorting thereto to ensure that the most advantageous prices for the government are obtained.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensures that economy and efficiency when resorting to any alternative methods of procurement. In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under Section 47 of the General Appropriations Act and adopt control measures to ensure that the employee’s monthly net take home pay not be lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P 4,000.00) after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.

We have recommended that Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer see to it that supporting documents are complete before certifying and effecting payment. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds was strictly recommended.

We have reiterated that the management to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We have recommended that the officials concerned submit the reports on time. Strict compliance as to the prescribed period relative to the submission of monthly reports is to be observed.

We have reiterated that the municipality enforce through the Bids and Awards Committee the procurement of commonly used supplies and equipment from the PS-DBM in compliance with the above-cited DBM Circular and Administrative Order.

We reiterate that the management observed that every time that there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract/purchase orders and their supporting documents, as well as notices of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to perform the conduct of physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a copy of the report to the audit team. To facilitate the conduct of physical count of properties, the inventory committee may consider performing physical count of newly acquired properties before moving to those that were acquired in the prior years. Inventory tagging should also be made in order to prevent double counting.

We have reiterated that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers in order for the Accounting Unit to have proper basis in setting up the amount of RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year to present fairly the balances of these accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the unutilized balances of CDF be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the national government by remitting the said fund to the Bureau of Treasury in full compliance to Supreme Court Ruling dated November 19, 2013 or reconcile the account and make necessary adjustments in the books as the case may be.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the above cited provisions in the handling of government funds. Collections should be deposited in full and intact daily to safeguard its funds from the risks of loss or theft and possible misuse.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the procurement of goods and services such that competitive public bidding be adopted in all its procurement activities The Bids and Awards Committee, who has the responsibility for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR, should strictly observe the provisions on the use of alternative mode of procurement before resorting thereto to ensure that the most advantageous prices for the government are obtained.

We have recommended that the accountable officers adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 73 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I and Section 101 of PD 1445 to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in their custody are kept diligently.

We have recommended that the agency compel the contractor to accomplish the project as soon as possible, otherwise, the agency should impose the sanctions cited in paragraphs 8.3 and 8.4 of Annex “E” of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions in the books of accounts in accordance with accounting rules and regulations. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that transactions are charged to the proper accounts in order to show the correct account balances in the financial statements. Appropriating Capital Outlay Budget for each office is also recommended.

We have recommended that the management to refrain from paying non-health personnel that are not bound by their duty nor directly involve in providing health services in compliance with PhilHealth Circular 10 s. 2012.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under the General Appropriations Act and adopt control measures to ensure that the employees monthly net take home pay not be lower than required minimum take home pay after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Bids and Award Committee together with the BAC Secretariat and the Technical Working Group Members to religiously observe and adhere to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184 and with Section 4, paragraph 6 of Presidential Decree 1445. Also, they should invite in writing, at least three observers from Commission on Audit, from duly recognized private group, and from Non-Government Organization.
Further we have recommended that copies of all contracts, preparatory requirements, and their supporting documents should be submitted to our office in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended the municipal mayor to establish specific guidelines and policies in the grant of all kinds of financial assistance to its constituents in order to charge the financial assistance in their proper classifications.

We have reiterated that the management observe that every time that there is unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as of yearend, it should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code: 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund books.
We have recommended that the Petty Cash Fund Custodian shall strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 that prescribed the manner and timing of PCF replenishment.

We have recommended that the local government unit create/strengthen a GAD Focal Point System who will coordinate, guide and monitor the development and implementation of the agency’s GAD Plan and GAD-related programs, projects and activities.

We have recommended that the municipal officials should exert extra effort to maximize its revenue raising power to accumulate additional funds to support projected expenditure of the municipality which might be beneficial to its constituents.
We have recommended that the Management undertake immediate settlement of the audit disallowances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the difference and make the necessary adjustment to present accurate balances of the reciprocal accounts Due to other Funds and Due from Other Funds.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  that Municipal Accountant make necessary notices to concerned offices for the submission of SSMI together with the corresponding RIS which shall be the basis in preparing JEV in the dropping of the subject inventory items. Likewise, trace all the pertinent documents pertaining to prior year’s transactions and make necessary adjustment to present the accurate balances of the Inventory accounts.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management require the:
Municipal Accounting Office to update recording on the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) to support more accurately the balances of the PPE accounts in the financial statements;
property officer to maintain a complete and updated Property Cards, trace source documents pertaining thereto, and regularly conduct reconciliation with the Accounting office so that any discrepancy noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled; and
concerned offices to exert concerted efforts to conduct the required annual physical count or inventory taking of all property, plant, and equipment of the Municipality, submit the corresponding inventory report thereon using the prescribed format, and reconcile property records against the physical inventory report so that any discrepancy noted could be immediately corrected and adjusted accordingly.
We recommended that municipality return the unutilized DAP and PDAF totaling ₱75,610.34 in compliance with Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
We recommended that the management strictly follow the rules in granting cash advances provided in COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, require the Accountant to (a) limit the grant of special cash advances to officials other than the designated special disbursing officers; (b) require bonding of special disbursing officers; (c) limit the payment against cash advances to ₱15,000.00 and to transactions that are petty and are impractical to be paid in check; (d) require the settlement of cash advances as soon as the purpose for it was drawn has already been served;  (e) send written notices/demand letters to concerned officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to enforce its settlement; and (f) require the submission of supporting documents for reimbursable RATA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that management cause the publicity on all its program/projects/activities in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund only to eligible expenditures; and (b) advise the Local Development Council to perform due diligence in the review of the budget to ensure the proper funding source of identified high-impact or capital expenditure projects for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that management (a) fully comply with the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b) and continue effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the members of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as stipulated in Section 1 of R.A. No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160, Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-A, March 14, 2000; and (b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF.

We recommended that Management utilize Gender and Development Fund on programs/projects and activities in accordance with the mandates of Republic Act No. 7192 and PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-1 to all government agencies to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant ensure that the net take home pay of employee shall not be less than the required ₱3,500.00 per month in compliance with Section 48 of the GAA FY 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that management settle over-due obligations to BIR and encourage them to continue complying with the withholding and remitting of taxes in accordance with the existing revenue regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure that all activities are conducted in the most efficient and economical manner; (b) submit the lacking documents for the activities undertaken; and (c) observe rules and regulations governing the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommended the incumbent management to adhere to the rules and regulations provided in the COA Circular No. 2012-003.
